snake_length_count: How many cells long is the snake, including the yellow head and all connected body cells?
Count the total length of the snake. Include the head and every connected body cell.
How many board cells are occupied by the snake, counting the head and tail?
Count every visible snake cell, including the head, body, and tail. What is the total?
What is the snake's total length in cells, including its head and tail?
safe_direction_count: {snake_rule_text} How many of the four next moves (up, down, left, right) are safe?
From the current head cell, count the directions among up, down, left, and right that are safe. {snake_rule_text}
{snake_rule_text} Count the safe next-move directions from the snake head.
How many immediate directions can the snake move without hitting the board edge, its body, or a gray wall cell? {snake_rule_text}
{snake_rule_text} Report the number of safe one-step directions from the head.
path_result_option_label: {snake_rule_text} Follow these moves in order: {planned_moves}. Which labeled option shown in the image is the result? Point options mark possible final head cells; the GAME OVER option means the snake hits the board edge, its body, or a gray wall cell. {planned_move_wall_annotation_rule_text}

Simulate the listed moves from the current board: {planned_moves}. {snake_rule_text} Report the image option letter for the result. Point options are final head cells; GAME OVER means a hit before the moves finish. {planned_move_wall_annotation_rule_text}
{snake_rule_text} The planned moves are {planned_moves}. Choose the labeled option in the image that matches the result: a marked point cell or GAME OVER. {planned_move_wall_annotation_rule_text}
Trace the head through these moves: {planned_moves}. {snake_rule_text} Which option letter drawn in the image gives the result? {planned_move_wall_annotation_rule_text}
{snake_rule_text} After applying {planned_moves}, choose the visible option label for the final point or GAME OVER result. {planned_move_wall_annotation_rule_text}

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_square_grid: a Snake game board with a yellow snake head, connected body cells, red food, and gray wall cells
code_prompt_defaults.snake_rule_text: A move is safe when the head stays inside the board and does not enter the snake body or a gray wall cell. Moving onto red food counts as safe.
code_prompt_defaults.planned_move_wall_annotation_rule_text: For a game-over result, use boxes around the visible in-board head path cells up to the hit cell or board edge.
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_snake_length_count: set "annotation" to bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] around every snake-occupied cell, including the head and tail
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_snake_length_count: set "answer" to the total number of snake-occupied cells as an integer
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_safe_direction_count: set "annotation" to bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] around each destination cell that is safe for the next move; include the food cell if it is safely reachable
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_safe_direction_count: set "answer" to the number of safe next-move directions as an integer
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_path_result_option_label: set "annotation" to bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] around the in-board cells the head traverses until the result; for an off-board hit, use the visible path cells up to the board edge
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_path_result_option_label: set "answer" to the option letter shown in the image, one of "A", "B", "C", or "D"
